formulas.name: 1. Break-Even Analysis
formulas.formula: Break-Even Point (BEP) = Fixed Costs / (Selling Price - Variable Costs)
formulas.variables: Fixed Costs, Selling Price, Variable Costs
formulas.notes: To determine the point at which a business becomes profitable

formulas.name: 2. Cost-Volume-Profit Analysis
formulas.formula: Profit = Selling Price - (Variable Costs + Fixed Costs)
formulas.variables: Selling Price, Variable Costs, Fixed Costs
formulas.notes: To analyze the relationship between costs, volume, and profit

formulas.name: 3. Return on Investment (ROI)
formulas.formula: ROI = (Gain from Investment - Cost of Investment) / Cost of Investment
formulas.variables: Gain from Investment, Cost of Investment
formulas.notes: To evaluate the return on an investment

formulas.name: 4. Payback Period
formulas.formula: Payback Period = Cost of Investment / Annual Cash Inflows
formulas.variables: Cost of Investment, Annual Cash Inflows
formulas.notes: To determine the time it takes to recover an investment

formulas.name: 5. Internal Rate of Return (IRR)
formulas.formula: IRR = Rate at which Net Present Value (NPV) equals zero
formulas.notes: To evaluate the profitability of an investment

formulas.name: 6. Net Present Value (NPV)
formulas.variables: Cash Inflows, Rate, Time, Initial Investment
formulas.notes: To evaluate the present value of future cash flows

formulas.name: 7. Entrepreneurship Development Index
formulas.notes: To measure the level of entrepreneurship development in a region

formulas.name: 8. Entrepreneurial Motivation Index
formulas.formula: EMI = (Number of Entrepreneurs with High Motivation / Total Number of Entrepreneurs) × 100
formulas.variables: Number of Entrepreneurs with High Motivation, Total Number of Entrepreneurs
formulas.notes: To measure the level of entrepreneurial motivation

formulas.name: 9. Venture Capital Funding
formulas.notes: To determine the amount of funding required for a venture

formulas.name: 10. Innovation Index
formulas.formula: II = (Number of Innovations / Total Number of Firms) × 100
formulas.variables: Number of Innovations, Total Number of Firms
formulas.notes: To measure the level of innovation in a region
